interventionCandidates.barrier: Friction at the moment of action blocks capable, motivated users
interventionCandidates.title: Pre-fill the blocking step
interventionCandidates.description: Pre-populate the field that forces a context switch so the first action takes seconds, not minutes. Fully reversible behind a flag.

apease.notes: Smallest reversible test of the dominant barrier.

interventionCandidates.barrier: Friction at the moment of action blocks capable, motivated users
interventionCandidates.title: Defer the blocking step to after first value
interventionCandidates.description: Move the high-friction step until after the user has seen value, testing whether ordering alone lifts completion.

interventionCandidates.barrier: No environmental cue, so the behaviour depends on memory
interventionCandidates.title: Anchor a cue to an existing weekly routine
interventionCandidates.description: Attach a single lightweight reminder to a moment the group already attends to, rather than adding a new channel.

apease.notes: FLAG: confirm reminder frequency with comms to avoid notification fatigue.

interventionCandidates.barrier: The new behaviour is invisible, so social proof runs the wrong way
interventionCandidates.title: Show an aggregate 'most people' signal
interventionCandidates.description: Surface an honest aggregate count of peers performing the behaviour on a shared surface.

interventionCandidates.barrier: The new behaviour is invisible, so social proof runs the wrong way
interventionCandidates.title: Publish individual peer activity by name
interventionCandidates.description: Make each peer's activity individually visible to the cohort to maximise social proof.

apease.notes: FAIL on Acceptability and Equity: exposes individuals without consent and disadvantages less-active users. Parked regardless of score.

faq.q: Will pre-filling the field cause wrong data?
faq.a: We only pre-fill values the system already holds with high confidence, and users can edit before confirming.

faq.q: Is the cue just another notification people will mute?
faq.a: It's a single reminder anchored to an existing routine, capped in frequency, and measured against opt-outs during the test.

gapLog.content: Resource envelope assumed conservative (one engineer, two weeks, no new spend) — confirm with owner.
Success threshold for the cue test depends on the week-8 metric that is not yet instrumented.
